summary: Tāne ascends to the summit of the heavens to receive the three kete of knowledge — kete-aronui, kete-tuauri, and kete-tuatea — and the sacred stones. His brother Whiro opposes him the whole way, hurling swarms of insects, reptiles, and carrion birds to cast him down; Tāne climbs on with the winds' aid and wins the baskets. Refused the treasures, Whiro is driven down to the underworld to wage endless war on life.
reenactment_hook: Take the high/ascent route and recover three separate hidden intel caches (boss dossiers, maps, weakness files) while an enemy floods you with harassment waves, then win the run using the knowledge rather than brute force.

epithet_grants.epithet: Tāne te wānanga (Tāne of sacred knowledge)
epithet_grants.trigger: recover all three intel caches under harassment on the ascent route and clear the final boss using their intelligence, on a VIP table or higher

sources: Best, Some Aspects of Maori Myth and Religion (NZETC)
Te Ara — Encyclopedia of New Zealand (public recension only)

title: Lono kills Kaikilani and founds the Makahiki games in her memory
summary: Playing kōnane with his wife Kaikilani, Lono hears a stranger hail her as a lover and, in a jealous rage, strikes her dead. Overcome with grief, he roams the islands like a madman, challenging every man he meets to box and wrestle, and institutes the Makahiki games in her honor before departing by sea with a promise to return.
reenactment_hook: After losing an ally on the run, publicly dedicate a formal contest — a duel, arena minigame, or wager-match — to them on camera, and win it in their name.

epithet_grants.trigger: win a formal contest dedicated on camera to a fallen ally after their death, on a VIP table or higher

sources: Malo, Hawaiian Antiquities ch. 36 (Emerson tr. 1903, PD)

title: Tāwhaki and Karihi destroy the Ponaturi at dawn
summary: The Ponaturi sea-goblins have murdered Tāwhaki's father Hema and enslaved his mother. Guided by her, Tāwhaki and his brother Karihi seal every chink of the goblins' sleeping-house and hold the door shut through the night, keeping them convinced it is still dark. At Tāwhaki's word they throw the house open to the fatal sunlight, and the whole host perishes at once.
reenactment_hook: Rather than fight a light- or element-vulnerable swarm head-on, seal their only retreat and withhold their weakness — keep the room dark — until the entire host is trapped inside, then trigger it all at once (throw the doors to the dawn) for a total wipe.

epithet_grants.trigger: wipe a vulnerable enemy host in a single exposure by sealing their retreat first, on a VIP table or higher

title: Tāwhaki climbs the vine to heaven by following every instruction
summary: At the hanging vines that reach to the sky, Tāwhaki's brother Karihi seizes a loose, swinging root and is flung away and lost. Tāwhaki grasps the fixed root and climbs, obeying every ritual instruction to the letter; he reaches the heavens, wins back his celestial wife Tangotango and their daughter, and lightning flashes from his armpits.
reenactment_hook: Clear an ascent gauntlet by honoring every optional ritual or preparation prompt exactly and in order — choosing the safe 'fixed root' at each fork — where a rushing rival who grabs the wrong hold falls.

epithet_grants.trigger: reach the summit of an ascent gauntlet honoring every ritual prompt with zero shortcuts, on a VIP table or higher

title: The slaying of the Pouākai
summary: A giant man-eating bird, the Pouākai, carries people off from a South Island community to its mountain eyrie. The warrior Te Hau-o-Tawera and fifty men build a strong trap of manuka saplings by night; he offers himself as live bait to draw the bird's dive, and when it strikes and tangles itself in the lattice, the warriors spear it to death.
reenactment_hook: Down an untargetable flying boss not with damage but by building a trap from gathered materials and volunteering a contestant as live bait — lure the dive into the snare, then kill it in the grounded-and-entangled window.

epithet_grants.trigger: ground and kill a flying boss using a self-built trap and live bait, at any table

title: The Menehune build the Alekoko fishpond in one night
summary: Commissioned to raise a great stone wall for a fishpond at Alekoko on Kauaʻi, the Menehune work only in darkness, passing stones hand to hand down a line miles long, on the condition that no one watch them and the work finish before dawn — paid a single shrimp each. When they are spied upon, they abandon the wall forever, leaving two gaps unfinished.
reenactment_hook: Complete a large construction or objective covertly in a single window with no camera or enemy observing you — accept a cheap fixed price and the rule that any observation voids the whole thing, and deliver it before the deadline unseen.

epithet_grants.trigger: complete an unobserved overnight build objective without being watched, at any table

title: Taʻaroa breaks his shell and makes the world from himself
summary: In the Tahitian cosmogony, Taʻaroa — the supreme aspect of the ocean god (Tangaroa's archipelago skin) — exists alone inside his shell Rumia, spinning in the endless void. Bored in the dark, he cracks the shell and climbs out into nothingness; from one half he raises the dome of the sky, and from his own body — spine to mountains, ribs to hills, feathers to trees — he forms the land and all living things. Creation is the god turning himself inside out.
reenactment_hook: Break out of a confined starting state and build the arena from your own substance — permanently spend your own stats, HP, or equipment to raise terrain, spawn cover, or generate resources that the whole team then uses to win.

epithet_grants.trigger: win a run in which you built the decisive terrain or resources by permanently sacrificing your own stats, on a VIP table or higher

title: Hine-tītama flees to the underworld and becomes the Great Woman of Night
summary: Hine-tītama, the Dawn Maiden, is the daughter Tāne made and then took as wife while she did not know he was her father. When she learns the truth, shame drives her down to Rarohenga, the underworld, and no plea from Tāne can bring her back. She tells him to raise their children in the world of light while she waits below to draw those same children down to her in death — and so becomes Hine-nui-te-pō, guardian of the dead.
reenactment_hook: Uncover a hidden, devastating truth about your run or your patron, then permanently renounce every revival or return option and take a one-way descent role — trading your former self for command over death's rules for the rest of the run.

epithet_grants.epithet: the Great Woman of the Night
epithet_grants.trigger: learn a hidden betrayal, permanently renounce all revival/return, and win the run wielding the death-keeper role, on a VIP table or higher

title: Tūmatauenga stands alone against the storm and takes utu on his brothers
summary: When Tāwhirimātea assaults his brothers with storms, only Tū stands firm — feet planted in the earth, weathering the tempest unbroken while the others flee. Afterward he takes revenge on the brothers who abandoned him: he snares Tāne's birds, nets Tangaroa's fish, and digs up the food-children of Rongo and Haumia, eating them all and stripping their tapu so they become noa — common food for humankind.
reenactment_hook: Stand your ground under an overwhelming assault without retreating a single tile while allies fall or flee, survive it outnumbered, then hunt down and finish every enemy that abandoned the fight — claiming their resources as your own spoils.

epithet_grants.trigger: win a fight outnumbered without retreating a tile, then land the killing blow on every remaining enemy, on a VIP table or higher

sources: Grey 1855, 'The Children of Heaven and Earth' (PD)

title: Lono returns with the Makahiki: the season of peace and games
summary: Each year Lono returns from the sea as the god of rain and harvest, and for four months war is kapu across the islands. His image, the akua loa — a tall pole hung with white kapa — is carried sunwise around each island to gather tribute, while the people give themselves to boxing, wrestling, sledding, and surfing under his peace, wagering heavily on the champions. When the season closes, Lono departs again over the water.
reenactment_hook: Declare an on-camera truce window and hold it — clear a full objective segment with zero kills while collecting tribute or resources — then convert the ceasefire into a wagered contest and profit from it before combat resumes.

epithet_grants.trigger: hold a self-declared truce window to completion and win the wagered contest that follows, at any table

sources: Malo, Hawaiian Antiquities ch. 36 (Emerson tr. 1903, PD)

title: Tiʻitiʻi breaks Mafuiʻe's arm and wins fire for Samoa
summary: In the Samoan telling, fire is kept below the earth by Mafuiʻe, god of earthquakes and fire (Mahuika's archipelago skin). Tiʻitiʻi — the Samoan Māui — descends and wrestles him beside the fiery cavern; seizing the god's arm, he wrenches it off and threatens to break the other unless Mafuiʻe yields fire to humankind. Beaten, Mafuiʻe reveals that fire lives hidden in certain trees, and thereafter it is drawn by rubbing dry wood. Earthquakes are Mafuiʻe stirring with his one remaining arm.
reenactment_hook: Win a boss's guarded secret by force rather than trickery — grapple a resource-keeper into submission, permanently cripple it (destroy one of its actions or limbs), and extort the hidden method (a boss's true weakness or a locked recipe) as the price of sparing its life.

epithet_grants.trigger: defeat a fire or resource-keeper boss by force and extort its hidden method from it, on a VIP table or higher

sources: Turner, Samoa a Hundred Years Ago 1884 (PD)

title: Eshu and the cap that broke a friendship
summary: Feeling neglected by two inseparable friends who farmed adjoining fields, Eshu walked the boundary between them wearing a cap red on one side and black on the other. Each friend saw only the one color, and when they later argued over the stranger's cap they came to blows and destroyed their friendship. The lesson is that whoever ignores Eshu will find even perfect allies set against each other.
reenactment_hook: Turn two enemies (or rival contestants) against each other by feeding them contradictory-but-individually-true information, let them fight, and strike only the weakened survivor -- winning an encounter you never engaged directly.

epithet_grants.trigger: turn two enemies or rival contestants against each other with split information and defeat the survivor, on any table

sources: Yale-New Haven Teachers Institute unit 98.02.04 (two-colored hat)
Ayodele Ogundipe, Esu Elegbara scholarship; Ellis 1894 (PD)

title: Orunmila, witness of every destiny chosen
summary: Before birth, each soul kneels before Olodumare to choose its ori -- its destiny -- and Orunmila alone is present to witness the choosing. Because he saw every person select their fate, he is Eleri Ipin, the Witness of Destiny, and can read anyone's odds and unfolding through Ifa divination. Nothing about a life is uncertain to him, which is exactly why the house needs its one incorruptible oddsmaker.
reenactment_hook: Acquire true foreknowledge before acting: obtain the win condition of every boss on the floor before first contact and clear the entire floor without a single blind engagement, acting only on the intelligence you gathered.

epithet_grants.trigger: enter every boss encounter on a floor already knowing its win condition and clear the floor with no blind engagement, on a VVIP table
do so across three consecutive runs without a single unknown engagement

title: Orunmila withdraws and leaves the sixteen palm nuts
summary: Offended by his own children, Orunmila withdrew from the world and ascended to heaven on a palm tree, leaving behind sixteen sacred palm nuts (ikin). Cast upon the opon Ifa tray and read by his priests, these let humanity still consult him and read destiny, so his wisdom stayed accessible though he himself had departed. He sacrificed his presence to leave a permanent oracle behind.
reenactment_hook: Permanently spend a valuable resource (a skill charge, an action economy, or your own remaining participation) to plant a reusable, run-long true-outcome oracle the whole party can consult, then win the floor by acting on its readings.

epithet_grants.trigger: permanently spend a valuable resource to leave the party a run-long true-outcome oracle, then win the floor using it

title: Obatala, drunk on palm wine, shapes imperfect bodies
summary: Charged with molding human bodies from clay, Obatala drank palm wine and, his hands unsteady, shaped some people with crooked limbs, blindness, and albinism. When he sobered and saw what he had done, he swore never to touch palm wine again and took all such people under his special protection as his sacred ones. So human imperfection entered the world, and the flawed became holy.
reenactment_hook: Win a run under self-imposed discipline: clear the floor carrying a permanently disabled body part (never healing it), using no intoxicant, frenzy, or judgment-clouding buff, and carry an injured ally to the exit alive.

epithet_grants.epithet: Oba Ala (King of the White Cloth)
epithet_grants.trigger: win a run carrying a permanently disabled body part from start to finish, using no intoxicant or frenzy buff, on a VIP table
and carry an injured ally to the exit alive in the same run

sources: Oxford Reference, 'Obatala Is Tempted with Palm Wine'

title: The making of dry land, lost to Oduduwa
summary: Olodumare sent Obatala down with a snail shell of earth, a five-toed hen, and a palm nut to create dry land upon the primordial waters. Obatala drank palm wine and fell asleep on the way; his brother Oduduwa took the materials, descended at Ile-Ife, and loosed the hen, whose scratching spread the soil into the world's first land. So Oduduwa became founder of the earth and of Ile-Ife, and Obatala forfeited the honor of creation to his own lapse.
reenactment_hook: Seize a contested creation objective a hesitating rival was set to claim: reach and activate the floor's central anchor or founding point before the contestant who holds the 'rightful' claim, converting their delay into your foundation.
sources: Yoruba creation myth, gateway-africa.com; Idowu, Courlander

title: Oba Koso -- the king who did not hang
summary: Shango, an Alaafin of Oyo, tested a charm that drew lightning from the sky, and the bolt struck his own palace, killing his wives and children. Disgraced and abandoned, he withdrew to Koso and, in grief, was said to have hanged himself -- but his devotees denied it, crying 'Oba ko so!', 'The king did not hang!', and he rose transfigured into the orisha of thunder. Total ruin, wagered and lost, converted into godhood.
reenactment_hook: Go all-in on a Forsaken table and stake something existential (a permanent stat, your contract, an existence token), suffer the catastrophic on-camera loss, then reclaim victory by maxing the audience/hype meter -- the crowd's cry that converts a ruined loser back into a god.

epithet_grants.epithet: Oba Koso (The King Did Not Hang)
epithet_grants.trigger: lose an all-in Forsaken stake on a VVIP or Forsaken table, then win the run with a maxed audience/hype meter
and land the winning blow during an active lightning or storm field

title: Gbonka and Timi, the generals set against each other
summary: Fearing his two invincible war-generals, Timi and Gbonka, Shango set them against each other, ordering Timi to capture Gbonka and then demanding a public trial by combat. Gbonka defeated and humiliated Timi, then turned on Shango himself, challenging the king before his own court and driving him toward the ruin at Koso. The house that pits its champions against each other may find the survivor turned against the throne.
reenactment_hook: Engineer a duel between two elite enemies or rival champions, let them fight to a finish, then defeat the weakened victor -- winning an encounter impossible to take by direct force.

epithet_grants.trigger: cause two elite enemies to duel each other and defeat the weakened survivor, on a VIP table

sources: en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oba_ko_so (Gbonka-Timi duel; underlying legend traditional, Ladipo's 1963 opera is modern IP -- do not quote the play)

title: Ogun cuts the road for gods and men
summary: When the orishas first tried to descend to the newly made earth, an impenetrable primordial thicket barred the way and their soft tools failed. Only Ogun, first of the orishas (Osin Imole), possessed iron; with his machete he hacked open the road so all the others could pass. For giving iron and the cleared path to gods and humans alike, he is honored as the pioneer of civilization.
reenactment_hook: Be the trailblazer for everyone: using a bladed or metal weapon, be first through every sealed door or obstacle on the floor and open a new route or shortcut that other contestants then use.

epithet_grants.epithet: Osin Imole (First of the Primordial Orishas)
epithet_grants.trigger: be first through every breached obstacle on a floor and open a shortcut other contestants use, on a VIP table
and clear the whole run using only bladed or metal weapons

title: Ogun's rage at Ire
summary: Returning from war parched, Ogun -- king of Ire -- found the townspeople keeping a ritual of silence and could get no answer or drink from anyone. Enraged, he drew his sword and slaughtered his own people before he realized they had been forbidden to speak. In grief and shame he turned the sword on himself, or sank into the earth (versions vary), vowing to answer only those who call him rightly -- the origin of his terrible reputation and strict observance days.
reenactment_hook: Clear an entire encounter in one unbroken frenzied assault -- enter a berserk/frenzy state and wipe a full room without stopping -- then survive the self-inflicted backlash cost that follows.

epithet_grants.trigger: clear a full room in a single unbroken frenzied assault and survive the self-inflicted backlash

title: The seventeenth orisha, without whom creation stalls
summary: Olodumare sent seventeen orishas to organize the world, but the sixteen males ignored and excluded the one woman, Oshun. Everything they attempted failed -- rains stopped, the earth went barren, nothing they built held. When they returned to Olodumare he told them nothing succeeds without her; they begged her pardon, she relented, the world came right, and from her intervention came the birth of Ose-Tura.
reenactment_hook: Prove the run cannot be won without the overlooked: route the deciding objective through the party's least-powerful member (the support or lowest-stat contestant lands the win), or, solo, resolve by charm and negotiation the exact gate that brute force stalled on.

epithet_grants.trigger: win a run in which the deciding objective is completed by the party's least-powerful member, or (solo) resolve the floor's hardest gate by charm with zero damage, on a VVIP table

sources: worldhistory.org/Oshun; cosettepaneque.com (public secondary retellings; verse content not mined per Ifa closure)

title: The covenant of Oshun and Osogbo
summary: When the founders of Osogbo settled by the Osun river and accidentally offended the goddess, Oshun appeared and struck a pact: she would protect the town and grant fertility to its women if they honored her each year. The bond is renewed annually at the Osun-Osogbo festival in the sacred grove, where the Arugba carries offerings to the river to keep the founding covenant alive.
reenactment_hook: Strike a binding protective pact with a place or NPC community and uphold it for the whole run: accept a recurring obligation (make an offering at a fixed shrine each floor), never miss it, and win under the location-wide protection the maintained pact grants.

epithet_grants.trigger: maintain a per-floor tribute pact to a shrine unbroken and win the run under its protection

sources: worldhistory.org/Oshun (pact details to re-verify against the UNESCO nomination text)

title: Babalu-Aye's exile and return in power
summary: Struck with disease and driven from the orishas' court in disgrace, Babalu-Aye wandered the roads as a scarred, crippled beggar attended only by his dogs. In exile he mastered both plague and cure and returned so feared and so revered that his very name became unspeakable, and the pantheon that cast him out now sends tribute down to him. Versions differ across Yoruba, Arara, and Lucumi tradition (a divination, a reconciliation, a healing by another orisha).
reenactment_hook: Begin from nothing and outlast: start a run at critical health or stripped of gear, carry an active infected/poison condition through an entire floor without curing it, and win by endurance on an outlast/survive win condition rather than by killing.

epithet_grants.trigger: win a run begun at critical health while carrying an uncured infection to the end, on a VVIP or Forsaken table

sources: CDC Museum, Shapona; David H. Brown, Santeria Enthroned (academic)

title: Olokun's contest, lost to a chameleon
summary: Proud of her matchless weaving and dyeing, Olokun challenged the supreme god to a contest of splendor to prove herself superior. Olodumare sent only his servant, the chameleon Agemo, who instantly matched color for color every gorgeous cloth Olokun donned. Realizing that if the god's mere messenger could effortlessly mirror her finest work she could never best the god himself, Olokun conceded -- and the sea has bowed to the sky ever since.
reenactment_hook: Defeat a stronger enemy without matching its power: beat a boss or rival by copying and reflecting its own attacks and patterns back at it (a mirror strategy), taking it down through mimicry and adaptation rather than out-damaging it.

epithet_grants.trigger: defeat a stronger boss by reflecting its own power back at it, without out-damaging it, on a VVIP table

title: Aje, daughter of the deep, brings up the cowries
summary: Aje, orisha of wealth, is reckoned a daughter of Olokun, lord of the sea's bottomless riches. From her father's sunken vault come the cowrie shells that served as the Yoruba's money, rising from the deep into human hands. Where Aje chooses to dwell, wealth accumulates, and the unexpected gift of a large tiger cowrie is the sign she has taken personal interest in someone. (Thin, variant-rich corpus; her individuation and gender vary by source -- provisional.)
reenactment_hook: Turn the deep's raw material into profit: descend to the run's lowest reachable level, strip its wealth, and exit having multiplied your currency (loot low, sell high), ending the run richer than you entered.

epithet_grants.trigger: end a run with far more currency than you entered, having stripped the deepest reachable level

sources: Ellis 1894 (PD), Aje Shaluga as wealth orisha
africanpoems.net, Salute to Aje (diaspora-practice tier -- provisional per dossier honesty flag)

title: The Ozidi Saga -- vengeance under the sorceress's hand
summary: Ozidi is the posthumous son of an assassinated warrior, born to avenge his father. Under the direction of his fearsome sorceress grandmother Oreame -- who arms him with charms and buffs him from the sidelines -- he hunts down and kills each of his father's murderers in turn. The saga climaxes when the Smallpox King, Engarando, arrives by sea to afflict him, and Ozidi prevails only when the disease is treated as an ordinary illness and cured, so the god withdraws defeated.
reenactment_hook: Run a vengeance ladder: defeat a sequence of named elite bosses one after another (one per target), buffed by an external handler feeding you support from outside the arena, and finish by defeating the plague-boss not with damage but by curing / mundane-izing its disease.

epithet_grants.trigger: clear a full vengeance ladder of named bosses under an external handler and defeat the final plague-boss by curing rather than out-damaging it

sources: Okpewho, 'Performance and Plot in The Ozidi Saga', Oral Tradition 19/1 (2004), open access (do not quote Clark-Bekederemo's in-copyright text)

title: How Anansi bought all the stories
summary: Anansi asked Nyame, the sky god, to sell him all the world's stories. The price was four impossible captures -- Onini the python, the Mmoboro hornets, Osebo the leopard, and the Mmoatia forest fairy -- and in Rattray's recorded Akan version Anansi added his own mother, Ya Nsia, to the stake. By trickery he took every one, delivered them to Nyame, and won the stories, so that from then all tales are anansesem, spider stories.
reenactment_hook: Win a capture-gauntlet with a zero-kill win condition -- subdue a set of dangerous creatures using traps and trickery, never killing them -- after first staking something of real living value (a companion, or a beloved permanent asset), and win the run to redeem the stake.

epithet_grants.trigger: complete a zero-kill capture gauntlet after staking a living companion or a permanent asset you value, and win the run to redeem it

sources: Rattray, Akan-Ashanti Folk-Tales (1930, PD in US), via multoghost.wordpress.com (avoid Gaiman/Marvel modern-IP renditions)

title: The covenant of Mami Wata
summary: Mami Wata offers a mortal sudden wealth, beauty, and healing in exchange for a covenant: exclusive devotion and secrecy, a 'spiritual marriage' in which the devotee must keep her identity hidden and take no other patron. While the pact holds she lavishes fortune; the moment it is broken -- the secret told, another lover or god taken -- every gift reverses and the devotee is ruined, often drawn down into the water. Variant-rich by design.
reenactment_hook: Hold an exclusive secret pact under active temptation: accept a single patron's covenant, keep that patron's identity hidden through the whole broadcast (deflect every on-camera question), refuse every rival god's better mid-run offer, and win the run with the pact intact.

epithet_grants.trigger: win a run having kept an exclusive secret patron-pact unbroken against at least one rival god's temptation, on a VIP table

sources: Drewal, 'Mami Wata', African Arts 41/2 (2008), open PDF

title: Eshu frames the High God and becomes his messenger
summary: Eshu stole yams from the High God's garden, then wore the god's own sandals to walk through the plot and leave the god's footprints among the theft, slyly suggesting the god had robbed his own garden in his sleep. To settle who truly knew what happened on earth, the exasperated High God ordered Eshu to climb to heaven every night and report all that passed below. So the trickster's con made him the indispensable messenger between earth and sky.
reenactment_hook: Con your way into an indispensable role by manufacturing false evidence: plant misleading-but-convincing evidence that shifts blame or credit (frame an enemy, fake a trail), and parlay the deception into a permanent run-long advantage -- a role, a key, an escort pass -- that you then win the floor with.

epithet_grants.trigger: win a floor after using planted false evidence to seize a lasting advantage (a role, a key, or an escort)

sources: encyclopedia.com, Eshu (yam-theft, sandal frame-up, ordered to report to the sky nightly)

title: Babalu-Aye is mocked at the dance and looses the plague
summary: Old and lame, Babalu-Aye (Sopona) came to a great celebration at Obatala's palace, and when he rose to dance he stumbled and fell. The assembled orishas laughed at him, and in fury he tried to strike them all with smallpox. Obatala stopped him and drove him out into the bush, where he has lived as a feared outcast ever since -- and where the mockers learned that scorning the humble invites the plague.
reenactment_hook: Turn a public humiliation into a spreading plague: after taking an on-camera debuff or mockery (a knockdown, shock, or taunt debuff), retaliate by inflicting a contagious infected/poison condition that spreads across an entire enemy group and wipes it out.

epithet_grants.epithet: Alapa-dupe (He Who Kills and Is Thanked)
epithet_grants.trigger: after suffering an on-camera debuff, clear an entire enemy group with a single spreading infected or poison affliction

sources: en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sopona (mocked at the dance, tries to infect them, driven to the bush)
oriire.com, 'Sopona as an Agent of Divine Order' (web-verified 2026-07-18)

title: Olokun's flood and the seven chains
summary: Angered that dry land and humanity had encroached on her domain, Olokun sent the sea against the shore while Obatala rested in the sky, flooding the low country and drowning many. On Orunmila's counsel, Ogun forged the longest chain ever made; Obatala descended into the ocean and bound Olokun with seven chains until she swore to abandon her design of destruction. She remains moored in the deep, and when her rage stirs the chains, the floods return -- so people never forget to honor her.
reenactment_hook: Defeat a rampaging, un-killable force by binding it rather than beating it: face a flood or environmental boss you cannot out-damage, discover its restraint condition (forge or find a chain, seal the flood-valves, immobilize it), and win by containment -- a discoverable-win-condition boss straight from the myth.

epithet_grants.trigger: defeat an un-killable flood or rampage boss by binding and containing it rather than out-damaging, on a VVIP or Forsaken table

sources: en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Olokun (flood; bound with seven chains by Obatala)
curioustaxonomy.net Yoruba Flood Folklore; yorubaness.com.ng (Ogun forges the chain; web-verified 2026-07-18)
resolves dossier §4 Wave-3 watch item (chained-flood tradition now sourced)

title: Aje lifts Oshun out of poverty
summary: In a time of bitter poverty Oshun owned but a single dress, which she washed daily in the river. Aje (Aje Shaluga), orisha of wealth, saw her from the watery depths and, moved by her plight, rose bearing cowries and golden treasure -- in one telling filling the skirt of her dress with jewels and tinting her clothes the sacred yellow -- and set the goddess on the road from destitution to abundance. So wealth answers grace, and the tiger cowrie became Aje's sign of favor. (Diaspora-practice sourcing; Aje's gender and individuation vary -- provisional.)
reenactment_hook: Lift a destitute rival from ruin: give a large share of your own currency or a high-value item to the poorest contestant on the board (even an opponent) on camera, then still go on to win the run.

epithet_grants.trigger: give a large share of your currency or a high-value item to the board's poorest contestant on camera, then still win the run

sources: occult-world.com, Aje-Shaluga (rescues Oshun from poverty); en.oshaeifa.com Aje Shaluga; orisa.com.ng Aje Shaluga (diaspora-practice tier, web-verified 2026-07-18)

title: The comb and mirror left at the waterside
summary: Mami Wata is glimpsed at the waterside, combing her long hair before a mirror; startled, she dives away and leaves her comb and mirror behind. She comes to the finder in a dream, demanding her treasures back. The one who returns them and vows faithful devotion is rewarded with luck, wealth, and beauty; the one who keeps them is drawn down into the water, ruined or lost. The test is not the taking but the giving-back.
reenactment_hook: Give back the valuable thing you found instead of keeping it: recover a high-value item that secretly belongs to a hidden benefactor (a boss's or NPC's lost treasure), return it rather than pocket it, and win the run on the hidden covenant boon the return unlocks -- sacrificing a sure gain now for a greater hidden reward.

epithet_grants.trigger: return a hidden benefactor's high-value item instead of keeping it, and win the run on the covenant boon it grants

sources: en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mami_Wata (comb/mirror left behind, dream demand, reward or drowning)
EBSCO Research Starters, Mami Wata; oriire.com, 'Variations of the Myth of Mami Wata' (web-verified 2026-07-18)
